profile.behavior: Clings upright to trunks and large branches, bracing with stiff tail feathers; probes, scales bark, and excavates dead wood for insects.
field_marks: Medium-sized, boldly black-and-white woodpecker with an upright, straight-backed posture.
Long, straight, chisel-like bill, nearly as long as the head.
Two white facial stripes frame a black head; males show a small red patch on the rear crown.
Broad white patch runs down the center of the black back.
Black wings are checkered with white; outer tail feathers are plain white and stiff.
Dark eye and feet; long, pointed tail used as a brace on trunks.
profile.habitat: Mature deciduous, coniferous, and mixed woodlands with medium to large trees; also forest edges, groves, burned stands, and other treed habitats with dead wood.

sources.title: Hairy Woodpecker Identification — All About Birds
Hairy Woodpecker Life History — All About Birds

findings: No material issues identified. The illustrated adult male has one head, one long straight bill, two wings, two legs with gripping feet, and one stiff, pointed tail; its black-and-white pattern, white back patch, white wing checks, head stripes, dark eye, and rear-crown red patch agree with the field-reference photos and live sources.

The plate’s measurements, Picidae family assignment, behavior, habitat summary, palette, and field-mark text agree with the independently checked sources. Leuconotopicus villosus is an accepted scientific combination; some authorities instead use Dryobates villosus.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Hairy Woodpecker Identification — All About Birds
